page: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server powered by the QEMU hypervisor and AMD EPYC Genoa processors. It features 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU architecture includes a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and AES cryptography. The instance is configured with 31.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.95 GB of RAM per core, and includes 640 GB of local SSD storage. It does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 3035.28 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1968.0, both placing in the top 25%. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 48785.85 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 11377.0, falling into the middle 50%. This discrepancy highlights a tradeoff associated with the shared CPU allocation model, where resource contention may limit sustained multi-core scaling. Memory performance is robust, showing strong large-block read (277.56 GB/sec) and write (137.51 GB/sec) bandwidths, alongside a strong PassMark memory score of 2904.26.

The server demonstrates strong capabilities in specific computational tasks, such as PassMark CPU physics (5542.92) and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression (198.15 GB/sec). It also performs well in small-to-medium LLM inference tasks, achieving 275.06 tokens/sec for text generation on a 135M model and 39.54 tokens/sec on a 7B model. Given its shared CPU allocation and high vCPU-to-RAM density, the cpx62 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that require high burst capacity or strong single-threaded execution but do not demand continuous, dedicated multi-core utilization. Suitable workloads include software compilation, text and image processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 16 vCPUs and 16 physical cores powered by AMD EPYC Genoa processors running at 2.0 GHz under the QEMU hypervisor. The system is equipped with 31.0 GB of RAM, 640 GB of local SSD storage, and no GPU resources. It utilizes a shared CPU allocation model, which presents a performance tradeoff: while single-core benchmarks like Geekbench (1968.0) and stress-ng (3035.28) rank in the top 25%, multi-core metrics fall into the average range. Large-block memory bandwidth is strong, with reads at 277.56 GB/sec and writes at 137.51 GB/sec. This hardware profile provides a cost-efficient solution for bursty workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and small-to-medium LLM inference tasks that benefit from strong single-threaded performance without requiring dedicated, non-shared CPU resources.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 31.0 GB RAM, and 640 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance and cost-efficient burst capacity.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx62 is an x86_64 virtual server with 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 31.0 GB RAM, and 640 GB SSD storage for bursty workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server combining AMD Genoa processors, local SSD storage, and strong single-core performance for cost-efficient compute workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs and 31.0 GB of system memory
640 GB of local SSD storage included with no bundled GPU
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory bandwidth performance
Shared CPU allocation model optimized for cost-efficient burst workloads
Well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and light LLM inference
